seo_h1: Stesimbrotus of Thasos on Athenian Statesmen

seo.browser_title: Stesimbrotus of Thasos Historian on Eulogikon Greek Archive
seo.meta_title: Stesimbrotus of Thasos Ancient Greek Historical Works
seo.meta_description: Stesimbrotus of Thasos, a 5th century BCE historian and biographer. Explore his surviving fragments. Free to read and download from the Greek library archive.

biography_html:
Stesimbrotus of Thasos was a 5th-century BCE Greek writer from the island of Thasos. Ancient sources indicate he was active in Athens during the Peloponnesian War period, moving in its political and intellectual circles. The Byzantine Suda calls him a rhapsode and historian, suggesting he performed epic poetry in addition to writing prose.

Later authors, particularly Plutarch, cite him as a contemporary source for Athenian statesmen, though Plutarch also criticizes him for bias and slander, especially against Pericles, hinting at possible oligarchic sympathies. His known work is On Themistocles, Thucydides, and Pericles, a biographical study of the three Athenian politicians. It survives only in fragments preserved by later authors like Plutarch and Athenaeus.

Stesimbrotus is significant as an early practitioner of biographical writing in Greece, focusing on character and anecdote rather than political narrative. His fragments offer valuable, if contested, insights into the personalities of the Athenian elite and the political culture of Classical Athens.
